title: sagina maritima subsp. stricta
intro: Sagina maritima subsp. stricta, also known as strict sea pearlwort or saltwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its tiny white flowers and delicate, creeping stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of certain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sagina maritima subsp. stricta include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Sagina maritima subsp. stricta typically involve infusions or teas made from the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as a calming beverage or used topically as a skin salve.
